zh.lifeSummary: 當William Land III於1600年出生在英國英格蘭米德萊可時，他的父親William Land Sr.29歲，母親Ann Juret Wormall19歲。他於1614年8月1日與Marye Not在英國米德萊可斯特普尼聖 登斯坦 安德 哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1661年在英國英格蘭越可爾霍爾波利享年61歲。

nameMeanings.name: Land
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, and Dutch: topographic name from Old English, Middle Dutch land, Middle High German lant ‘land, territory’. This had more specialized senses in the Middle Ages, being used to denote the countryside as opposed to a town or an estate.
English: topographic name from Middle English launde ‘glade’ (Old French land), or a habitational name from a place called with this word, such as Launde in Leicestershire or Laund in Yorkshire.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of the three farmsteads so named, from Old Norse land ‘land, territory’ (see 1 above).

variants.name: Lander
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic or status name from Middle High German lant, German Land ‘land, territory’ (see Land 1). Compare Landmann .
German: topographic name from Middle High German lander ‘picket fence’.
German: from the personal name Lanther, see Landherr 1.

variants.name: Lands
meanings: English: variant of   Land  .

variants.name: Landt
meanings: German: variant of   Land   1.

variants.name: Lande
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads, so named from either the dative singular or the plural form of land (see Land 3).
German: from the old personal name Lando, a short form of ancient Germanic names formed with lant ‘land’ (see Land ).

French: topographic name for someone living on a heath, moor, French lande (from Gaulish landa ‘space, land’), or a habitational name from any of numerous minor places called La Lande from this word. Compare Lalande .

variants.name: Landfried
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Chantel.
South German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements land ‘land, territory’ (see Land ) + fred, frid ‘peace’. This surname is also found in France (Lorraine).

variants.name: Lawn
meanings: English:
variant of   Land   2.
from Middle English laun ‘lawn, fine linen’ (French laun, from the name of the city of Laon), perhaps for a maker or trader.

variants.name: Landa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jorge, Jose, Luis, Juan, Manuel, Enrique, Jesus, Julio, Mario, Roberto, Miguel.

Basque: topographic name from landa ‘(large) field, prairie’. There is a place of this name in Araba/Álava province, Basque Country (Spain), and the surname may also be a habitational name from this place.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads in southwestern Norway, so named from the plural of land ‘land, territory’ (see Land 3).

variants.name: Lant
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Land   with devoicing of the final consonant.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Land III was born in 1600, in Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Land Sr., was 29 and his mother, Ann Juret Wormall, was 19. He married Marye Not on 1 August 1614, in Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1661, in Horbury, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Land III est né en 1600, à Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Land Sr., avait 29 ans et sa mère, Ann Juret Wormall, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Marye Not le 1 août 1614, à Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1661, à Horbury, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 61 ans.

ko.St Dunstan and All the Saints, Stepney,: 영국 미들레세스 스테p니 세인트 던스탠 앤드 앨 세인츠 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: William Land III(이)가 1600년 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Land Sr.(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Ann Juret Wormall(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1614년 8월 1일 영국 미들레세스 스테p니 세인트 던스탠 앤드 앨 세인츠 c후르치에서 Marye Not(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1661년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 호르버이에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Marye Not em 1 de agosto de 1614, em Saint Dunstan and All Saints Church, Stepney, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. ru.St Dunstan and All the Saints, Stepney,: сеинт дунстань анд алл сеинц чурч, степни, миддлесекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Land III родился в 1600 г. в местности миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Land Sr. было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Ann Juret Wormall, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Marye Not 1 августа 1614 г. в местности сеинт дунстань анд алл сеинц чурч, степни, миддлесекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1661 г. в местности хаурбури, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 61 год.
